title: On macOS, Downloads, Documents, and Desktop are TCC-protected and deny CLI reads
rule: On macOS the Downloads, Documents, and Desktop folders are guarded by the system privacy layer, so a CLI process without Full Disk Access gets 'Operation not permitted' (EPERM) when it lists or reads them, even though the path exists and a test -d check passes. Disabling a tool's own sandbox does not help, and re-running in the same shell inherits the same denial; grant Full Disk Access to the terminal app, or move the files outside those folders.
triggers: ls returns Operation not permitted but the directory exists
test -d succeeds but find or ls fails with EPERM on macOS
a CLI tool cannot read a folder under ~/Downloads on macOS
Operation not permitted listing ~/Documents or ~/Desktop from a script
